subject: Ecology
title: Wetland Plants: Cattails and Reeds
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Wetland Plants: Cattails and Reeds.

hook.text: Cattails grow so fast they can take over a wetland—and that's both a gift and a problem.

buildup.text: Their dense stands provide cover for birds, filter water, and add organic matter to sediment.

discovery.text: Cattails spread by underground rhizomes, forming monocultures that shade out other plants.

twist.text: Excess nutrients from farms supercharge cattail growth, letting them dominate and reduce diversity.

climax.text: Managing cattails often means controlling nutrient runoff rather than cutting the plants.

punchline.text: Cattails reveal what's in the water.

quiz.question: What causes cattails to form monocultures in wetlands?
